description: §65.2 baseline — a single component-scope `#{}` rule (`.btn { color }`) targeting one element fires NOTHING. The no-false-positive floor: the conflict checker only speaks on an actual overlap. This is the clean-compile proof that the added checker is additive (§65.14) and does not perturb a well-formed scoped stylesheet.
rationale: §65.14: the §65.2 conflict-checker is ADDED on top of the KEPT §9.1 scoped `#{}` emission — new diagnostics only. A well-formed single-rule component scope trips neither the hard nor the soft diagnostic. The whole `E-STYLE-*` / `W-STYLE-*` family stays silent on clean code.
